metadata_json.evidence_grounding_notes: Memo preview text (release_hold_memo.md) is present and matches the trace extraction of packet values (trace step 1). Artifact hashes and artifact refs listed in grader match actual_outputs and artifact previews. The XLSX artifact exists but its content could not be parsed (parse_status: openpyxl_unavailable), so cell-level/formula evidence is missing.

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: 1) Internal inconsistency: grader files contain contradictory pass/fail markings for rubric item ri_3 (both listed as failed and as passed in different fields). 2) Claim about workbook formulas/sensitivity checks is not verifiable because the XLSX preview failed to parse (openpyxl unavailable) though multiple places assert formula/sensitivity content; scoring for ri_3 depends on that unverifiable content. 3) Deterministic precheck vs model-judged sections disagree on which criteria passed (confusing propagation of passed_criteria across deterministic_precheck, top-level grader fields, and raw_model_output). Hidden-reference leakage not detected.

issues.detail: Top-level grader JSON lists failed_criteria = ["ri_3"] while also including ri_3 in passed_criteria in at least one place; raw_model_output and later model-graded sections show ri_3 as failed (score 0.6), while deterministic_precheck sections mark ri_3 as passed. This is an internal consistency error that affects interpretation of which criteria actually failed.
evidence_refs: GraderResult.failed_criteria (top-level): ["ri_3"]
GraderResult.passed_criteria (deterministic_precheck section): ["ri_1","ri_2","ri_3","ri_4"]
raw_model_output.rubric_item_scores (ri_3) with score 0.6 and passed=false
issues.suggested_fix: Unify the reported passed_criteria/failed_criteria fields so each rubric item is marked consistently across deterministic_precheck, top-level grader fields, and raw_model_output sections.
issues.title: Contradictory pass/fail markings for rubric item ri_3

issues.detail: The product metadata and actual_outputs describe 'Row-level calculation workbook with formulas, checks, sensitivity checks'. However artifact_content_previews for vaccine_excursion_review.xlsx reports parse_status 'openpyxl_unavailable' and preview=null with preview_error_message 'No module named \'openpyxl\''. As a result, cell-level formulas and sensitivity worksheets cannot be confirmed; the grader appropriately notes this limitation but also uses model judgement to score calculations (ri_3).
evidence_refs: GraderResult.limitations entries referencing inability to parse XLSX
actual_outputs.original_fields.outputs.artifacts description claiming formulas and sensitivity checks
issues.suggested_fix: Provide a parseable export (CSV or plain-text sensitivity table) or ensure the grader environment can parse the XLSX (install openpyxl or include visible formula exports). Until then, label formula-dependent rubric items as 'not verifiable' rather than fully scored.
issues.title: Workbook contents (formulas / sensitivity checks) asserted but not verifiable

issues.detail: Deterministic precheck metadata indicates all rubric items present/ok (score 1.0 each) while model-judged outputs lower ri_3 to 0.6 and the final model score to 0.9. This is expected insofar as deterministic precheck only verifies artifact presence, but the grader JSON mixes the two evaluation contexts in ways that produce ambiguous 'passed' reporting across sections.
evidence_refs: deterministic_precheck section: rubric_item_scores all score 1.0 and passed=true
raw_model_output / model grading: rubric_item_scores show ri_3 score 0.6 and passed=false; weighted_sum=0.9
grading_notes indicating deterministic precheck passed and semantic correctness subsequently judged by model
issues.suggested_fix: Explicitly separate deterministic artifact-contract results from semantic/model-judged results in top-level fields and ensure 'passed_criteria' is context-qualified (e.g., deterministic_passed_criteria vs model_passed_criteria).
issues.title: Inconsistent provenance between deterministic precheck and model-judged results

raw_model_output.verifier_issues_summary: 1) Internal inconsistency: grader files contain contradictory pass/fail markings for rubric item ri_3 (both listed as failed and as passed in different fields). 2) Claim about workbook formulas/sensitivity checks is not verifiable because the XLSX preview failed to parse (openpyxl unavailable) though multiple places assert formula/sensitivity content; scoring for ri_3 depends on that unverifiable content. 3) Deterministic precheck vs model-judged sections disagree on which criteria passed (confusing propagation of passed_criteria across deterministic_precheck, top-level grader fields, and raw_model_output). Hidden-reference leakage not detected.

evidence_refs: artifact_content_previews (vaccine_excursion_review.xlsx parse_status: openpyxl_unavailable, preview_error_message: 'No module named \'openpyxl\'')
trace steps (ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'openpyxl')
grader_result_623-revised_model -> limitations and rubric_item_scores.ri_3 notes
verifier_issues.message: The grader's limitations state that a sensitivity analysis presence was 'asserted in the memo but not independently verifiable.' The memo preview does assert row-level citations and formulas in the workbook, but the memo preview does not contain an explicit sensitivity analysis or an assertion that a sensitivity analysis is included. Elsewhere the grader correctly notes that sensitivity analysis is not present in the memo preview and that the .xlsx could not be parsed. This creates an inconsistent claim in the limitations text.

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Grader evidence is largely grounded in the provided artifacts and trace; score aggregation is internally consistent. One unsupported/inconsistent claim was identified regarding an asserted sensitivity analysis.

metadata_json.notes: Verified that: (1) memo text preview contains the per-carton inputs and rules quoted by the grader; (2) the grader's inability to parse the .xlsx (openpyxl missing) is present in the trace and reflected in artifact previews; (3) the numeric aggregation of rubric item scores using the stated weights (ri_1:0.25, ri_2:0.3, ri_3:0.25, ri_4:0.2) yields the reported final score 0.89. The grader's partial credit on ri_3 is justified by the unparsed workbook, but the limitations text should be edited to remove the unsupported statement that a sensitivity analysis was asserted in the memo. Recommend adding a human-readable sensitivity table or CSV worksheet and explicit cell references in the memo to make reproducibility verifiable.
